message:
perf(pager): batch-allocate EOF pages to reduce mutex contention during concurrent inserts

Introduces a page lease mechanism for SimpleTransaction that
pre-allocates PAGE_LEASE_BATCH_SIZE (8) EOF pages in a single lock
acquisition. This reduces inner-mutex contention when concurrent
writers cause frequent B-tree splits that each need a new page.

How it works:
- page_lease: Vec<PageNumber> field added to SimpleTransaction
- First allocation in a transaction is always single-page to avoid
  over-reserving for short transactions
- Subsequent allocations (when allocated_from_eof or
  allocated_from_freelist is non-empty) batch-allocate 8 pages,
  keeping the first and storing the rest in page_lease
- page_lease.pop() is the fast path — no mutex acquisition needed
- Only applies to TransactionMode::Concurrent; non-concurrent
  writers always allocate one page at a time

Lease lifecycle:
- Unused lease pages are returned to the global freelist on
  commit, rollback, savepoint release, and Drop, ensuring no
  page numbers are leaked

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
